page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the legacy n1 server family. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor using x86_64 architecture with hyperthreading enabled. The instance is configured with 86.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-core execution is highly robust, with stress-ng multi-core performance and PassMark CPU extended instructions testing scoring in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with read-only and write bandwidth benchmarks consistently ranking in the top 25% across both small cached and large uncached blocks.

The primary tradeoff of the n1-highcpu-96 is its low memory density relative to its massive compute core count, which limits its utility for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory processing. However, it demonstrates strong capabilities in highly parallelized tasks, as evidenced by its top-tier multi-core stress-ng results and high throughput in Redis SET operations. This profile makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded CPU-bound workloads, such as software compilation, ray tracing, parallel data processing, and static web serving, where raw parallel compute scaling is prioritized over per-core clock speed or high memory capacity.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon processor, paired with 86.0 GB of memory. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance exhibits a clear performance tradeoff: single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench and stress-ng single-core, rank in the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core performance and memory bandwidth consistently place in the top 25%. This resource density profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized, CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is best suited for workloads such as multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and parallel data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 86 GB RAM. It features strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth but weak single-core speeds.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 86 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core-count virtual machine delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel, compute-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor
86.0 GB of system memory with a low 0.9 GB per vCPU ratio
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth
Weak single-core processing and text processing speeds
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware acceleration
Suited for multi-threaded compilation, ray tracing, and parallel processing
